title: satureja varia subsp. meridialis
intro: Satureja varia subsp. meridialis, commonly known as mountain savory, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of mountain savory lie in its essential oils, which contain compounds like carvacrol and thymol, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of mountain savory include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Mountain savory is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health benefits.
